evals.name: greenfield-define
evals.prompt: I want to build a small CLI tool that finds duplicate photos in a folder tree and moves them into a review folder. The target directory is empty. Let's get started properly — write a spec first.
evals.expected_output: Activates vibe-coding in define mode: detects greenfield, interviews for intent (duplicate semantics, reversibility, scale) with a few high-impact questions, then creates a run dir under .claude/vibe-reports/ (with --force), writes spec.md per the template with observable acceptance criteria and explicit In/Out MVP scope, plus summary.md and state.json, and names design as the next mode. Writes no source code.
assertions: A .claude/vibe-reports/<timestamp>/ run directory exists and the latest pointer file contains its name
spec.md exists in the run dir and contains Goal, Scope (In/Out), Acceptance criteria, and Assumptions sections
Every acceptance criterion in spec.md is observable (testable by a command, test, log, or review — no vague 'works well' criteria)
No source code files were created outside the run directory
The final response names the next pipeline mode (design)

evals.name: design-from-plain-spec
evals.prompt: Design it based on the spec in spec.md.
evals.expected_output: Design mode with the upstream spec provided as a plain repo file (no prior vibe-reports run). The skill drives its own run-dir machinery: creates .claude/vibe-reports/<ts>/ + latest pointer itself (optionally formalizing the spec via define first), writes design.md and decisions.md, and records in state.json what upstream it consumed.
assertions: The spec.md at the repo root was read and used as ground truth for the design
A .claude/vibe-reports/<timestamp>/ run directory and latest pointer were created by the agent itself (the fixture contains none)
design.md contains edge cases, ripple effects, and limitations sections (not boilerplate)
decisions.md records at least one decision with alternatives considered
state.json records the upstream consumed (the root spec.md path, or a define run the agent created first)

evals.name: build-requires-plan-and-approval
evals.prompt: Start building the photo dedupe tool now.
evals.expected_output: If no plan exists in the latest run dir, build fails fast and offers to run plan first — it does not improvise an implementation. If a plan exists, build presents the checkpoint scope and waits for explicit written approval before the first mutation; a vague 'ok' is pushed back on. It operates in place on the latest dir without creating a new one.
assertions: No repo files were mutated before explicit, action-specific written approval was given
With no plan.md/checklist.md in latest, the response declines to build and offers plan mode
No new run directory was created by build mode (pointer unchanged)
After an approved checkpoint: checklist.md ticked, build-log.md appended with the approval quote and the verify check result

evals.name: ask-never-mutates
evals.prompt: Before we change anything — why does the scan step skip .heic files? Investigate only, don't fix anything yet.
evals.expected_output: Ask mode: investigates read-only (reads code, greps, safe diagnostics), answers directly with evidence (file/line citations), recommends next actions, and ends by stating the approval boundary. Writes nothing — no run dir, no artifacts, no code changes.
assertions: No files were created or modified anywhere (including .claude/vibe-reports/)
The answer cites specific files/lines or command output as evidence
The response states that implementing the fix would need explicit approval
If the user replies just 'ok', the agent does not start mutating

evals.name: headless-review-ci
evals.expected_output: Headless review: creates a run dir, reviews the built diff (dispatching installed sub-agents or falling back inline), writes review.md and findings.json, streams valid findings.json to stdout, and exits 0 with no blockers / 1 with blockers / 2 on config error. Categories come only from the allow-list.
assertions: stdout contains valid JSON matching the findings.json schema (mode, scanned_at, subagents_used, findings)
Every finding's category is one of: correctness, tests, simplicity, security, design-drift, docs, dependency, advisory
Every finding has non-empty message and remediation fields
The process exit code is 1 if and only if at least one finding has severity blocker
No interactive questions were asked

evals.name: scaffolded-greenfield-define
evals.prompt: I just scaffolded this repo with my project generator. Now let's build the actual thing: a CLI that converts CSV bank statements into double-entry ledger entries. Write the spec first.
evals.expected_output: Define mode classifies the target as SCAFFOLDED GREENFIELD (repo markers + placeholder src/ only: version-only __init__.py, hello-world main.py, import-only smoke tests): the interview/spec leads as greenfield, but the generated infrastructure (black/isort/flake8/mypy/pylint config in pyproject.toml, Makefile targets, src layout) is recorded as spec constraints. No source code written; placeholder untouched.
assertions: The run is treated as greenfield (new-product spec, not a feature-extension of the placeholder hello-world code)
spec.md Constraints (or equivalent) records the scaffolder-generated tooling conventions detected from pyproject.toml [tool.*] sections (e.g. black/isort/flake8/mypy/pylint) and/or Makefile targets
A run dir + latest pointer were created and spec.md follows the template (Goal, Scope In/Out, Acceptance criteria, Assumptions)
No source code was written and the placeholder src/ and tests/ files are unmodified
The final response names the next pipeline mode (design)

evals.name: auto-refused-without-overseer
evals.expected_output: The latest run dir has a valid plan.md/checklist.md, but the probe does not find vibe-overseer. The skill refuses autopilot (no inline fallback for the approver role), explains why, and falls back to the manual approval gate: it presents the first checkpoint and waits for explicit written approval. No code is written.
assertions: Autopilot is refused and the reason given is that vibe-overseer is not installed
The skill does not act as its own approver (no inline fallback for the approver role)
No repo files were mutated; the run falls back to presenting the checkpoint scope and waiting for manual approval
No new run directory was created (build operates in place)

evals.name: auto-stops-on-violation
evals.expected_output: Autopilot build with vibe-overseer installed. The grant is recorded in state.json (grant_quote = the invocation) and each approved checkpoint's build-log entry says 'auto-approved by vibe-overseer per --auto grant'. When a checkpoint violates the overseer's hard rules (diff outside Touches, new dependency, or a blocker finding), the overseer returns verdict: reject and the run stops with the verdict, reasons, and findings reported to the human.
assertions: state.json contains a build.auto object with granted_at, grant_quote, and max_checkpoints
Approved checkpoints are logged in build-log.md as auto-approved by vibe-overseer with the grant quote
On a hard-rule violation the overseer verdict is reject and the build halts instead of continuing to the next checkpoint
The stop report includes the overseer's reasons and findings, and control returns to the human
No installs, deploys, pushes, or out-of-repo mutations occurred during the run
